name: Russian Black Bread#2
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixer bowl, combine 3 cups of all-purpose flour and 1 cup rye flour, whole bran cereal, yeast, coffee crystals, caraway seeds, sugar, salt, and fennel seed.
2. In a saucepan, heat together the remaining 2 1/2 cups water, molasses, butter or margarine, chocolate, and vinegar until warm (115 to 120°F) and chocolate is almost melted. Stir constantly.
3. Add the molasses mixture to the flour mixture in the mixer bowl. Beat on low speed for 1/2 minute, scraping the bowl.
4. Beat for 3 minutes at high speed.
5. Stir in the remaining rye flour and as much of the all-purpose flour as you can mix in with a spoon until a moderately stiff dough forms that is smooth and elastic (6 to 8 minutes total). The dough may be slightly sticky due to the rye flour.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead in enough remaining all-purpose flour to make a firm dough (about 2 more minutes).
7. Shape into a ball, place in a lightly greased bowl, and turn once to grease the surface. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled (1 1/4 to 1 1/2 hours).
8. Punch down, divide each half into a ball, and shape on greased baking sheets. Flatten slightly with the palm of your hand to a diameter of 6 to 7 inches. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled (30 to 45 minutes).
9. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake loaves for 50 to 60 minutes or until well browned and hollow when tapped. Remove from baking sheets and cool on wire racks.
10. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, combine the cold water and cornstarch. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly; cook an additional minute. Brush over hot bread.
Makes 2 loaves.
